description: Fault-isolate cron scheduling so one malformed schedule string is skipped and logged (SCHED_INVALID) instead of crashing the entire cron-runtime. Harness-infra only: scripts/cron-runtime.ts, scripts/__tests__/cron-runtime.test.ts, crons/README.md.

userStories.title: Add a pure isValidSchedule helper
userStories.description: As the cron runtime, I need a side-effect-free way to test whether a schedule string is a valid cron expression so I can reject bad schedules before constructing a Cron.
acceptanceCriteria: Export isValidSchedule(schedule: string): boolean from scripts/cron-runtime.ts

Implement with a try/catch probe: construct new Cron(schedule) with NO callback function and NO name option (croner v9.1.0 has no static Cron.validate); call .stop() on the probe instance before returning true; return false in catch
isValidSchedule never throws for any string input, never schedules or fires anything, and leaves no live timer or registry entry behind
isValidSchedule('0 * * * *') === true; isValidSchedule('not-a-cron') === false; isValidSchedule('') === false
Unit tests cover a valid expression, a malformed string, and the empty string
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Skip and log invalid-schedule crons at load time
userStories.description: As the cron runtime, I want a cron whose schedule is invalid to be excluded from the scheduled set and logged, so one bad cron file does not poison the entries list handed to main().
acceptanceCriteria: loadCrons excludes any cron whose schedule fails isValidSchedule from its returned entries

loadCrons signature becomes loadCrons(dir?: string, logFn?: typeof log) with an optional second param defaulting to the module-private log, mirroring onJobError(id, err, logFn = log); existing loadCrons(tmp) call sites stay valid

Validation+skip+log runs AFTER parseCronFile returns a non-null entry and OUTSIDE the existing filesystem try/catch (lines 53-58); the unreadable-file path stays silently skipped, the invalid-schedule path logs SCHED_INVALID (paths stay distinct)
On skip, logFn is called with first arg = cron id (frontmatter id or filename-derived), second arg exactly 'SCHED_INVALID', third arg containing the offending schedule string
parseCronFile remains side-effect-free (no logging inside it); reloadBody and existing parser tests stay pure
loadCrons never throws on an invalid schedule

Tests: (a) a temp-dir cron with a malformed schedule is excluded from loadCrons output and does not throw; (b) a valid cron alongside the invalid one is still returned; (c) injected logFn receives the SCHED_INVALID call matching the assertion contract
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Fault-isolate the new Cron() loop and log a boot summary
userStories.description: As the cron runtime, I want each new Cron() construction in main() isolated so any residual constructor throw stops only that one cron, and a boot summary so a skipped cron is visible.
acceptanceCriteria: Each new Cron(...) construction in main() is wrapped in try/catch; on throw it logs SCHED_INVALID for that cron id (with the error) and continues the loop so remaining crons are still constructed (defense-in-depth; US-002 already filters at load time)

Boot logs log('system', 'BOOT', '<N> scheduled, <M> skipped') preserving the BOOT status token; counts are accurate with no double-counting (a cron dropped at load time is not also counted at construction)
With one invalid and two valid crons present, the two valid crons are scheduled, the runtime does not exit early, and the BOOT summary reads '2 scheduled, 1 skipped'

A test exercises the defense-in-depth catch by bypassing the load-time filter (e.g. stubbing isValidSchedule to return true for a malformed schedule, or testing an extracted construction helper directly)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Document the SCHED_INVALID token in crons/README.md
userStories.description: As an operator editing cron files, I want the SCHED_INVALID liveness token documented so I can recognize a skipped cron in crons/.cron.log.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a SCHED_INVALID row to the existing status-tokens table in crons/README.md (table present at ~lines 43-58) with an accurate one-line description
Add a sentence noting an invalid schedule: string is skipped at load time and never crashes the runtime, so other crons keep running
All existing status-token rows remain present and unchanged; only the SCHED_INVALID row is added (no reformatting of other rows)
Typecheck passes
